One thing I’m appreciating even more: the disconnect between…

profile_bio.description: The open credit network for the world

retweeted_tweet.text:
Spending two weeks in Singapore, Hong Kong, Tokyo and Seoul.

One thing I’m appreciating even more: the disconnect between Asia’s distribution and Western financial institutions. Today, Wall Street still struggles to access Asian distribution at scale, while Asia has limited access to Western financial infrastructure/products. 

Both sides have edges @Morpho’s open credit network can bridge.

Wrote a piece on what I think is one of the most underrated security issues in DeFi rn: custody risk at the protocol level.

The noncustodial stack, including code immutability and governance minimization, will be one of the most important factors in making DeFi infra more secure and preventing hacks like the ones we’ve seen these past few weeks.
